response:
The cheapest credible alternatives to Mixpanel are typically **open-source / self-hosted tools** like **OpenPanel**, **PostHog (self‑hosted)**, **Matomo**, and **Countly**, plus **Google Analytics 4** and a few “lite” SaaS tools, depending on how Mixpanel‑like you need the product analytics to be.[1][2][6][7]

Below is a concise breakdown so you can pick based on cost vs. capabilities.

---

## 1. Ultra‑cheap or free options

### OpenPanel (open source, Mixpanel‑style)

- **What it is:** An **open‑source product analytics** platform explicitly built as “the open‑source alternative to Mixpanel.”[1]
- **Pricing:**
  - **Self‑hosted:** Free with **no event limits** (you only pay your infrastructure costs).[1]
  - Managed SaaS: **starts at ~$2.50/month**, far below typical Mixpanel pricing.[1]
- **Pros:**
  - Very low cost for Mixpanel‑style funnels, retention, cohorts.[1]
  - Good fit if you’re price‑sensitive and OK with a younger ecosystem.
- **Cons:**
  - Less mature brand and ecosystem than PostHog/Amplitude.

---

### PostHog (open source / SaaS)

- **What it is:** All‑in‑one **product analytics + session replay + feature flags + A/B testing**, positioned explicitly as a Mixpanel alternative.[2][5][7]
- **Pricing:**
  - **Cloud:** Has a **solid free tier for up to ~1M monthly events** (good for early‑stage).[2]
  - **Self‑hosted:** License is free; you pay infra (PostHog estimates **~$500/month** once you’re at meaningful scale).[2]
- **Pros:**
  - Can replace multiple tools (Mixpanel + feature flags + A/B testing + replay).[2][5][7]
  - Open source and can be self‑hosted for data control/compliance.[2][5]
- **Cons:**
  - Self‑hosting is not “free” in practice once traffic grows due to infra + engineering time.[2]
  - Cloud pricing may ramp like Mixpanel once you exceed the free tier.

---

### Google Analytics 4 (GA4)

- **What it is:** Free analytics from Google; traditionally web‑focused, but can do some event‑based/product analytics.[6][7]
- **Pricing:** **Free for most use cases**; paid only if you go into BigQuery/storage/enterprise stack.[6][7]
- **Pros:**
  - Extremely cheap and well‑known.
  - Good for top‑of‑funnel, marketing, basic event tracking.[6][7]
- **Cons:**
  - Not a full Mixpanel replacement for deeper product analytics (cohorts, retention, complex funnels are more limited/awkward).[6][7]
  - Privacy and data‑ownership concerns for some teams.

---

### Matomo (formerly Piwik)

- **What it is:** **Privacy‑focused web & product analytics**, listed as a Mixpanel alternative.[6]
- **Pricing:**
  - **Self‑hosted:** Free (you pay infra).
  - Cloud has paid plans; typically cheaper than enterprise‑level Mixpanel for comparable traffic.[6]
- **Pros:**
  - Strong on privacy, self‑hosting, and compliance (GDPR‑friendly).[6]
  - Good if you mostly need web/product analytics without heavy advanced features.
- **Cons:**
  - Less specialized for modern product analytics (behavioral cohorts, experiment workflows) than tools like Mixpanel/Amplitude/PostHog.

---

### Countly

- **What it is:** **Open‑source, real‑time mobile & web analytics platform**.[3]
- **Pricing:**
  - Community/open‑source edition for self‑hosting (infra cost only).[3]
  - Paid enterprise with more features.
- **Pros:**
  - Focus on mobile analytics and user behavior.[3]
  - Self‑host for cost control and data ownership.
- **Cons:**
  - More configuration/maintenance vs SaaS tools.
  - UI/UX and features may feel less “plug‑and‑play” than Mixpanel.

---

## 2. Mid‑priced, closer equivalents to Mixpanel

If you still want a rich product analytics experience but are hoping for better pricing or value at scale, these are the main contenders.

### Amplitude

- **What it is:** One of the main **Mixpanel‑style product analytics** competitors; deep behavioral cohorting and predictive features.[2][4][6][7]
- **Pricing:** Event‑based pricing; at scale often **$2k–$5k/mont
